Data Pipeline Architecture
WHS/RTL Source
→
Bronze Layer
→
Silver Layer
→
Gold KPIs
→
Power BI / BI Layer

Architected and owned end-to-end distributed PySpark ETL pipelines on Azure Databricks for a global sportswear brand's Retail and Wholesale supply chain data. The 1.8 TB table (15 billion rows and growing) processes daily wholesale and retail dataset using medallion architecture. Achieved massive performance gains by applying Z-Order clustering, Deletion Vectors, and deep Spark execution plan optimization — reducing daily shuffle from 1.8 TB to 300 GB and rows copied from 1.8 billion to 250 million. Pipeline runtime cut by 1–2 hours per run.

Azure Databricks PySpark Delta Lake Z-Order Deletion Vectors Unity Catalog ADLS Gen2 Power BI Databricks Workflows Jenkins
Career

Professional Journey

Senior Data Engineer
LTM Limited
Client: Adidas · Noida, India
Aug 2021 – Present
  • Architected and owned distributed PySpark ETL pipelines on Azure Databricks, processing multi-terabyte retail and e-commerce datasets daily across the Medallion Architecture.
  • Optimized a 1.8 TB / 15 billion row real-time WHS/RTL supply chain pipeline — reduced daily shuffle from 1.8 TB to 300 GB and rows copied from 1.8B to 250M using Z-Order, Deletion Vectors, and Spark execution plan tuning.
  • Achieved 60–70% infrastructure cost reduction and 1–2 hour runtime improvements per pipeline run.
  • Delivered end-to-end data pipelines across Retail, Wholesale, and E-commerce domains enabling 5+ critical business KPI areas.
  • Migrated 200+ legacy SQL scripts to Spark SQL on Databricks and engineered Delta tables with final-layer views for Power BI reporting.
  • Maintained and enhanced 10+ production pipelines orchestrated via Databricks Workflows, ensuring data correctness and stability.
  • Automated manual data ingestion processes, driving significant reduction in operational effort and engineering efficiency.
